description_long: This object is a classic stovetop moka pot, commonly used for brewing espresso-style coffee. It is composed of three main sections: a lower chamber for water, a middle funnel-shaped filter for ground coffee, and an upper chamber for collecting brewed coffee. The body is predominantly black with a metallic finish on the internal filter and spout, and it features a faceted, polygonal design typical of Italian moka pots. The handle is side-mounted, angular, and made of a heat-resistant material, while the lid is hinged and octagonal with a small knob for easy lifting. The spout is short and integrated into the upper chamber, designed for precise pouring. The overall appearance is utilitarian and robust, with no visible decorative patterns.
description: A black, faceted stovetop moka pot with a hinged lid, side handle, and metallic filter components. The design is utilitarian and robust, with a short pouring spout and no decorative patterns.
description_view_0: The moka pot is shown from an angled top-down view, highlighting the polygonal lid and the separated filter and base components.
description_view_1: A side view with the lid open, clearly displaying the interior of the upper chamber, the handle, and the alignment of the filter and base.
description_view_2: A top-down view with the lid open, showing the internal structure and the separated filter, as well as the spout and handle placement.
description_view_3: A direct front view with the lid open, emphasizing the spout, handle, and the vertical alignment of all three main components.
attribute_lid_design: hinged octagonal lid with small knob
